channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: 6 Takeaways From Arizona’s Tense, Trump-Dominated Primaries
item.description: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Andy Biggs of Arizona, who was endorsed by President Trump, easily won the Republican primary for governor over Representative David Schweikert.

item.title: Election Denier Wins G.O.P. Nomination to Be Arizona’s Top Election Official
item.description: State Representative Alexander Kolodin will take on Adrian Fontes, the incumbent Democratic secretary of state, after defeating a more moderate Republican in the primary.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen
item.media:description: State Representative Alexander Kolodin at the State Capitol in Phoenix last year.

item.title: Trump Pick Wins G.O.P. Nomination to Challenge Arizona Governor
item.description: Representative Andy Biggs, a conservative Republican and an ally of the president, will try to unite Republicans against Gov. Katie Hobbs in a crucial swing state.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault
item.media:description: Representative Andy Biggs debated his Republican opponents in Scottsdale, Ariz., in June.

item.title: Saudis Reach Deal With U.S. That Could Let Them Enrich Nuclear Fuel
item.description: U.S. lawmakers and Israeli officials have expressed opposition to such a plan, to be announced Wednesday, fearing that the kingdom could use a civilian nuclear project to develop nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Edward Wong and William J. Broad
item.media:description: President Trump shaking hands with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud at the White House last November.

item.title: House Narrowly Passes Stopgap Spending Bill, but Shutdown Fight Looms
item.description: Republicans managed to push through legislation to fund the government through Dec. 4. But with Democrats opposed, the stage is set for a broader battle.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.media:description: Speaker Mike Johnson at the Capitol on Tuesday.

item.title: Wildfire Threatening Pleasanton Prompts Evacuation Order for Thousands
item.description: One firefighter was injured as a fire spread quickly across dry hillsides in Northern California, forcing some residents to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla and Francesca Regalado
item.media:description: The Little fire in Northern California, which has prompted evacuation orders.

item.title: Trump Lifts Ban on Direct Flights to Lebanon
item.description: The prohibition had been in place since 1985, when it was imposed in response to Hezbollah’s hijacking of TWA Flight 847.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump with President Joseph Aoun of Lebanon at the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: With His Latest Threats, Trump Signals to Iran That He Is Willing to Escalate
item.description: President Trump is facing a critical moment in the war, which has failed to bring about the “unconditional surrender” he once demanded.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: “We’ll be hitting that area probably pretty soon,” President Trump said of Pickaxe Mountain on Tuesday. “And there’s not a thing they can do about it.”

item.title: Jury Deadlocks on Murder Charges Against 2 Men in California Mass Shooting
item.description: The defendants were convicted of weapons charges in connection with the 2022 shootout in Sacramento, which left six people dead and 13 others injured outside a downtown arena.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai
item.media:description: A crime scene investigator taking photographs of evidence markers at the scene of the shooting in Sacramento, Calif., in 2022.

item.title: Small Plane Crashes Into House Near Houston
item.description: The plane, a Piper J3C-65, struck the house in Fulshear, Texas, around 6:45 a.m. on Monday, the authorities said. Its pilot was in serious condition on Tuesday, a hospital spokesman said.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

item.title: Hegseth Estimates Iran War Has Cost $37.5 Billion
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is requesting as much as $70 billion in emergency military spending after nearly five months of war.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: “This is a new request based on new realities of a world we face, stepping up to meet that moment,” Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Tuesday.

item.title: Troy Jackson Tightens Hold on Democratic Senate Nomination in Maine
item.description: State Democrats released a ballot for their nominating convention on Saturday that includes Mr. Jackson and two long-shot candidates with little public support from delegates.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner
item.media:description: Troy Jackson appears almost certain to emerge as the winner at the Maine Democratic Party’s nominating convention on Saturday in Bangor.

item.title: Senate Panel Advances Jay Clayton as Next Spy Chief on Party-Line Vote
item.description: If the full Senate confirms him as expected, Mr. Clayton will take over the Office of the Director of National Intelligence from Bill Pulte, a housing official with little national security experience.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz
item.media:description: Jay Clayton during his confirmation hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Wednesday.

item.title: Roger Rogoff Files Suit Challenging Trump’s Power to Fire Him
item.description: In the first lawsuit of its kind, Roger Rogoff argues that the Trump administration cannot fire him as U.S. attorney without first winning Senate approval for another candidate.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich and Danny Hakim
item.media:description: Roger Rogoff, a former federal prosecutor and state judge, in court in Seattle in 2016.

item.title: Trump Pushes Chinese Election Threat, but Files Show Russia’s Heavy Hand
item.description: Intelligence documents that the White House released to back up President Trump’s claims about China highlight Russian efforts to influence U.S. elections.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer and Dustin Volz
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates
Trump-Ukraine Whistle-Blower Complaint and Impeachment Inquiry
item.media:description: President Trump with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia in 2025. Mr. Trump has consistently resisted admitting U.S. findings that Russia has tried to interfere in American presidential elections.

item.title: Hegseth and Caine Will Appear Before Senators to Seek More Money for Iran War
item.description: The Pentagon seeks an additional $70 billion to cover the costs of the conflict and replenish depleted weapons stocks.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, left, and Gen. Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, testifying before a Senate committee in May.

item.title: Kaylee Hottle, ‘Godzilla vs. Kong’ Actress, Dies in Car Crash
item.description: A deaf teenage actress, she appeared in “Godzilla vs. Kong” and “Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire.”
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.category: Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire (Movie)
item.media:credit: Frederic J. Brown/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Kaylee Hottle at the premiere of “Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire” in Hollywood, Calif., in March 2024.

item.title: How Republicans Turned Olive Garden Into a Voter ID Talking Point
item.description: Republicans claim that Olive Garden polices its Never-Ending Pasta Bowl more tightly than the United States does its elections. It’s not that simple.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Gabby Jones for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Olive Garden in Manhattan. The restaurant chain has recently found itself at the center of a bitter political debate.

item.title: Trump Officials Withhold $1 Billion in Medicaid Funds From California and Minnesota Over Suspected Fraud
item.description: The administration accused California and Minnesota of fraudulent activity in their public health insurance programs.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kliff
item.media:description: Dr. Mehmet Oz and Robert F. Kennedy Jr., two top health officials, at a news conference on Tuesday.

item.title: Democrats Demand Information From Law Firm Over Its Deal With Trump
item.description: The demand from top congressional Democrats signals their plans to investigate the nature of law firms’ agreements with the Trump administration if they return to power after the midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Schmidt
item.category: Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP
item.media:credit: John Taggart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom agreed to do $100 million in pro bono work on issues that President Trump supports in a deal it reached with the White House.

item.title: Judges Reject Biden’s Effort to Keep Memoir Recordings Private
item.description: The former president is fighting the Justice Department’s effort to release tapes and transcripts.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.media:description: Former President Joseph R. Biden Jr. during a South Carolina Democratic Party fund-raising event in February.

item.title: Democrats’ Top Senate Candidate in Maine Won’t Support Schumer for Leader
item.description: Troy Jackson, who is set to become the party’s nominee, also said in an interview that he was not sure he wanted the support of Senator Chuck Schumer in the general election.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:description: Troy Jackson, a former president of the Maine Senate, has little time or money to build a campaign after Graham Platner’s unexpected exit from the Senate race.

item.title: Pentagon Identifies Soldier Killed and Another ‘Believed’ Killed in Iran War
item.description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, the 17th service member named, was killed during the disposal of a drone. The military also said Sgt. Angel S. Rampersad was “believed to have been killed in action.”
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin, Pooja Salhotra, Maia Spoto and Madaleine Rubin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton had been assigned to a unit at Fort Bragg in North Carolina.

item.title: Pan Am Clipper Endeavor Is Found After Over 70 Years Lost at Sea
item.description: Fifty-two of the 69 people aboard the plane died when it crashed off Puerto Rico and sank to the bottom of the Atlantic Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:credit: Pan Am Historical Foundation/John Johnson Collection
item.media:description: The Pan Am Clipper Endeavor, a Douglas DC-4, before its final voyage in 1952. The wreckage has been located off the coast of Puerto Rico.

item.title: Once Anti-Abortion, Now a Progressive: The Evolution of a Maine Democrat
item.description: Troy Jackson, who is expected to become his party’s nominee in the state’s marquee Senate race, has long been an economic populist, but his positions on social issues have changed markedly over time.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein and Beth Reinhard
item.media:description: Troy Jackson is expected to become Democrats’ new nominee for the U.S. Senate after his quick consolidation of support from party delegates last weekend prompted his leading rivals to withdraw.

item.title: What We Know About Tuesday’s Arizona Primaries
item.description: Arizona primary voters will choose nominees for governor, secretary of state and for key races that could determine control of the House.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault, Kellen Browning and Sejal Govindarao
item.media:credit: Jon Cherry for The New York Times
item.media:description: A polling site in Phoenix in 2024.

item.title: How a 911 Call Led to the Nation’s Strictest Trans Bathroom Law
item.description: People in Sandpoint, Idaho, don’t know how to characterize a woman’s brush with a transgender woman in a Y.M.C.A. locker room, but the emergency call that followed is still reverberating.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin and Loren Elliott For The New York Times
